_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning when and how to adapt our skills and strategies to prevent, calm down or resolve conflicts that happen within or between groups, across different contexts.

_source.w: We are learning when and how to adapt our skills and strategies to prevent, calm down or resolve conflicts that happen within or between groups, across different contexts.
y: Being able to adapt conflict resolution strategies to different contexts — school, workplace, community — makes us more versatile and effective problem-solvers.
Understanding how emotional complexity, language and power dynamics interact during conflict helps us navigate even the most challenging disagreements.
These advanced conflict management skills are essential for leadership, advocacy and maintaining healthy relationships throughout our lives.
eg: We research conflict management strategies appropriate for different contexts — home, school, community and workplace — considering how emotional complexity adds challenge.
We examine how language choices, listening skills and power dynamics can either help resolve or further entrench a conflict.
We analyse a complex real-world conflict and identify which strategies were used, which were effective, and what alternatives might have worked better.
We practise adapting our approach to conflict resolution when the context or power dynamics change — for example, mediating between peers versus raising an issue with an authority figure.
We role-play a workplace conflict scenario and develop a resolution plan that accounts for professional expectations and power differences.
We reflect on our own conflict resolution style, identifying strengths and areas for growth, and set goals for improvement.
